topic.what: First multivocal literature review synthesizing academic and industrial perspectives on convergence of Zero Trust security with IoT systems
topic.how: Systematic PRISMA-compliant review of 68 academic and 36 grey literature sources covering technical, organizational, and socio-technical dimensions
topic.why_matters: IoT adoption demands rethinking perimeter-based security; ZT offers continuous verification but IoT's constraints challenge implementation; no comprehensive synthesis existed

characteristics.context: Spanning 2017-2024, covering ZT and IoT security convergence topics

characteristics.value: 9 RQs across 3 levels and 3 activity types
characteristics.context: Concept/architecture, firms/industries, users/society levels for design, measurement, management activities
Socio-technical factors, measurement/validation, governance frameworks, standardization misalignment
First relevant paper in 2017, growth after 2021
IoT modifications, ZT architecture integration, blockchain, privacy, power IoT, attacks (AL only)
Academic: IoT compliance via modification. Industry: IoT integration into existing ZTA via NIST guidance

good_for: Understanding current state of ZT-IoT convergence

novelty: First multivocal literature review on ZT-IoT convergence
Comparative analysis of academic vs industrial epistemic approaches
Identification of 4 critical research gaps with socio-technical focus
Framework mapping RQs across activity types and analysis levels
open_problems: Cost-benefit evaluation of ZT-IoT convergence lacks empirical studies
Workforce competency alignment with NICE framework needed
Interdisciplinary collaboration gaps between academia and industry
Forensic and attack surface analysis for ZT-integrated IoT
In-field effective convergence requires joint academia-industry effort
Cryptographic agility and sustainable cryptography for constrained IoT
Governance and policy alignment across NIST, ISO 27001, EU Cyber Resilience Act
